---
name: hasToggle positioning decisions
description: Core positioning, identity thesis, voice architecture, and philosophical spine for hasToggle landing page rewrite
type: project
originSessionId: 18bff807-81b5-4d10-b38d-d4c2f0bf4c03
---
**Hidden North Star thesis (locked 2026-04-22, internal — not public copy):**

> hasToggle is a practice in ownership. Code is the proving ground. Language is the subject. AI generates the grammar of software. You learn to own the meaning.

Five-beat arc: identity → arena → subject → challenge → transformation. Staked words: *ownership*, *grammar*, *meaning*. Brand verb: *inhabit*.

**Product definition (locked 2026-04-23):**

> All-in on AI collaboration, proved through code.

- **Subject:** how to own your thinking when you work with AI
- **Arena:** building software
- **Proof:** code that embodies the practice or betrays it

Code is the moat. "Working with AI" as a standalone topic is saturated mush — taste/voice warfare against LinkedIn influencers. Code makes the practice testable: runs or doesn't, ships or fails, defends the practice or betrays it. Lose code rigor → become another prompt-wisdom pundit.

**Cohort implication:** Every "AI for developers" cohort graduates students with better prompts. hasToggle graduates students with better judgment — and code that proves it. Deliverable: working code embodying the practice, not prompts or mindset journals.

**What this rules out:** demos framed as dev-craft lessons with AI as decoration (React tutorials, optimistic-update deep dives). Mindset-only content that doesn't terminate in code. Web dev as subject rather than arena.

**Demo status (2026-04-23):** Demo 2 (Destructive Defaults / working-with-AI) locked — load-bearing, self-referential. Demos 1, 3, 4 up for re-think — Eric will brainstorm new demos that carry the AI-collaboration message better than rehooking existing dev-craft bodies.

**Philosophical spine:** Wittgenstein-1 (precision, formal language) = AI. Wittgenstein-2 (language-games, context, metaphor) = humans. AI produces sentences from outside; humans navigate meaning from inside. Grounded via Precht's Leuphana lecture (transcript at `docs/superpowers/specs/precht-transript.md`).

**Mechanism — three moves (locked):**
- Ask — a question reveals what you don't know; AI answers before you ask
- Collide — learning happens when your idea meets reality; AI lets you skip the meeting
- Rediscover — an answer you received is borrowed; an answer you worked out is yours

**Voice architecture (three layers):**
- Surface — plain, primitive, first-principles
- Meta — Pratchett-dry self-aware asides
- Demo — confrontational reveals

**Competitive stance:** Complementary to AI Hero, not competitive. AI Hero teaches tool operation; hasToggle teaches the thinking underneath. Code is the proving ground, not the boundary — vision scales to any domain that is a language-game.

**Why:** Eric believes AI will automate engineering discipline too. The last skill is judgment/meta-cognition rooted in linguistic ownership. hasToggle trains that.

**How to apply:** All landing page copy, content, and future features serve the thesis. Full spec at `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-01-landing-page-positioning-design.md`. Session context at `SESSION_CONTEXT.md` in repo root.

content.text:
Done. Values section now reads:

> **The mechanism**
> # Three moves.
> *Three moves. Not a framework, not a process. The smallest set of things you have to do for AI collaboration to produce ownership.*
>
> **I. Ask.** Probe what you don't yet know. AI fills the gap before you find it. We make you find it first.
>
> **II. Collide.** You learn when your idea meets reality. AI lets you skip the meeting. We don't.
>
> **III. Rediscover.** An answer you received is borrowed. An answer you worked out is yours.
>
> **The three moves build *conversance*. Fluency in the language of building software — enough to navigate the landscape, defend your thinking, and translate it for anyone. It's what people mean by *senior* but rarely name.**
>
> *AI produces the artifact. Only you hold the meaning.*

`page.tsx`, spec, and memory all updated. Conversance is staked as the end-goal word and paired semantically with *inhabit* in the brand vocabulary.

Demos next?

message.content:
Let's put this:
"The three moves build conversance. Fluency in the language of building software — enough to navigate the landscape, defend your thinking, and translate it for anyone. It's what people mean by senior but rarely name."

into a more active voice. Conversance, fluency, they're nouns. Let's make them do something instead. Turn them into verbs, make the sentence active. Tis first part and the last need rewriting. The middle part "navigate the landscape, defend your thinking, and translate it for anyone." is pretty good, it captures well what we have in mind and the consequence of the three moves.
<system-reminder>Respond with just the action or changes and without a thinking block, unless this is a redesign or requires fresh reasoning.</system-reminder>

content.text:
### Active-voice variants

1. The three moves teach you to speak the language of building software. You navigate the landscape, defend your thinking, and translate it for anyone. People call this *senior*. They rarely name it.

2. Run the three moves and you start to speak the language of building software — you navigate the landscape, defend your thinking, and translate it for anyone. People call this *senior*. They rarely name it.

3. Practice the three moves and you learn to speak code. You navigate the landscape, defend your thinking, translate it for anyone. People call this *senior* — they rarely name what they mean.

4. The three moves teach you to converse — to navigate the landscape, defend your thinking, and translate it for anyone. People point at this and call it *senior*. They rarely name what they're pointing at.

5. Three moves. Practice them and you start to speak code. You navigate the landscape, defend your thinking, translate it for anyone. People call this *senior*; they rarely have a name for what it is.

**My pick:** 2.

*"Run the three moves"* is the most kinetic opener. *"You start to speak the language of building software"* makes the verb carry the noun's weight (no more *fluency*, no more *conversance* — just speaking). The senior coda is two short active sentences.

Tell me to lock or iterate.

The end-goal statement names the destination of the three moves so the visitor doesn't have to infer it. *Conversance* is the staked end-goal word: not "skills," not "expertise," not "mastery" — *conversance*. It carries:
